title: Vietnamese Lemongrass Chicken

overview.description: Tender chicken thighs marinated with lemongrass, garlic and fish sauce before being grilled or pan-fried until beautifully caramelised.

ingredients: 2 stalks lemongrass, tender inner part only, finely minced
method: Combine the lemongrass, garlic, shallot, fish sauce, oyster sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, vegetable oil and black pepper.
Coat the chicken thoroughly in the marinade.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, preferably overnight.
Heat a griddle pan or frying pan over a medium-high heat.
Cook the chicken for 5–6 minutes per side until caramelised and cooked through.

planner.freezer-label: chicken freezes well in the marinade

learning.did-you-know: Lemongrass is one of the defining flavours of Vietnamese cooking, prized for its fresh citrus aroma rather than sharp acidity.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to prepare lemongrass, build a balanced Vietnamese marinade and caramelise chicken over high heat.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick, flavour-packed meal using inexpensive chicken thighs and pantry staples while introducing authentic Vietnamese flavours.
chapman-tips: Use only the tender lower third of the lemongrass stalk.
Marinating overnight gives the best flavour.
Don't move the chicken too often—allow it to caramelise before turning.
This also cooks beautifully on a barbecue.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze the chicken in the marinade before cooking for an easy future meal.
notes.leftovers: Slice and use in bánh mì, rice bowls or noodle salads.
